channel.title: Comments for page "Library Lifeline"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Library Lifeline"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Y'know, this has kind of lost its luster since Aldon left. It's like you went and changed Finnegan into a different character completely out of the blue so you could tell a totally different story for some reason, and you've more or less lost my interest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So after reading the story a second time, it was better. I think my problem was the articles you linked in the story, while extremely good, were also very large, which detracted from my initial read through because it made the story hard to follow. Reading the tale straight through without getting sidetracked the second time the story flowed much better. Now this could just be a thing I do, but it is something.
Other than that, I enjoyed the tale, and am interested to see where it wil continue. I find myself sad that Finnegan is slowly going away, but you are the writer, not I. I also enjoyed the background for the two, how they met.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some real nice background for both Finn and Aldon here. Origin stories can be a tricky business, but how you kind of had the two of them just end up friends is really fitting for the characters.

+1.

item.title: Now Playing Fourth Track
item.content:encoded:
Alright, here we go. Not entirely happy with it, but happy enough. Continuing from Killing Knowledge, the legend of a Nobody and the ending of Finnegan continues, to be concluded with the next tale.

Many thanks to Decibelle, Randomini, ihp, Conwell, Fae, EldritchCyanide, Goomy, and probably other people for helping me tweak this.
